prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: What My Sister Buried
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Court-Ordered Lifetime
Tagline: Every town has a basement.
Theme: Silence is just fear wearing cashmere.
Omen: a lake bell ringing without wind
Relationship dynamic: emotionally charged casserole exchange
Props: ceramic owl, casserole dish, unopened letter

Cast:
- Luke Hale | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=regional marketing executive | threat_aura=0.3 | space=marina tackle room described as paperwork I have not gone through yet | secrets=buried one traumatic detail under work for years; has an unsent email that would destroy a family
- Brooke Cross | archetype=rugged_scamp | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=widowed boat mechanic | threat_aura=0.43 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=helped someone disappear for compassionate reasons; had an engagement that ended under suspiciously quiet circumstances
- Evelyn Marlowe | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=bartender who knows everybody's business | threat_aura=0.46 | space=annex room described as seasonal materials | secrets=knows about a half sibling no one asked for; may die first or survive unbelievably
- Rachel Bennett | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=therapist | threat_aura=0.67 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=had brunch with someone officially deceased; knows why the old orchard chapel was closed
- Evelyn Hale | archetype=decorative_husband | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=attorney with no visible clients | threat_aura=0.48 | space=toolshed described as old family things | secrets=deleted a voicemail and regretted it spiritually; moved money during a family emergency
- Derek Marlowe | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=innkeeper | threat_aura=0.39 | space=side room with a space heater and old family records | secrets=accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it; saw the wrong person near the lake once and never shut up about it
- Wyatt Cross | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Daniel Hawthorne | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Brooke Cross is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Luke Hale says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Bellweather.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Brooke?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Wyatt. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Luke. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The ceramic owl is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Rachel."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."

- expanded_synopsis: string, 180-320 words
- scene_paragraphs: array of 6 strings, each 90-180 words, representing juicy HallTime scene expansion
